content.full_text:
tsb.LUY 
Influencing an Agency 
Assume that a 
son is a lobbyist employer: 
A. 
No portion of an employee's salary will be reportable 
unless that employee spends 10 percent or more of his 
compensated time during the month influencing legis-
lative or administrative action. 
B. 
Even if no portion of an employee's salary is reportable, 
the employer must report all other costs incurred for 
the purpose of influencing an agency which his lobbyist 
influences as a substantial or regular part of his 
business. 
C. 
If an agency is not being influenced by the employer's 
lobbyist, the agency nevertheless 
11 be deemed to 
have been influenced by the employer if an employee 
spends 10 percent or more of his time influencing 
all agencies spends a substantial or reg~lar part of 
h s time influencing the agency in question. 
D. 
If an agency is not being influenced by either the 
lobbyist or a 10-percenter, the staff has not decided 
whether the spending of a certain threshold amount of 
money in connection with influencing an agency will 
mean that the agency is being influenced pursuant to 
18600. 
For example if an employee spends 3 percent of 
his time influencing Agency X but spends $10~ $200 or 
$300 in that effort, is the agency being influenced by 
the employer? 
17D

sections.question_synthetic: The document addresses what constitutes reportable lobbying activity and costs when an employer has lobbyists influencing agencies, including salary reporting thresholds, reportable costs, and the circumstances under which an agency is deemed to be influenced.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: FPPC provides that salary is reportable only if an employee spends 10% or more of compensated time influencing legislative or administrative action; all other costs for influencing an agency must be reported regardless; an agency is deemed influenced if a lobbyist or 10-percent employee influences it, but the staff has not decided whether a monetary threshold applies when time spent is below 10%.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document fragment lacks clear question and conclusion section headers; appears to be an excerpt from a larger advice letter discussing agency influence reporting requirements under section 18600; missing formal letter structure and document metadata; formatted as advisory points A-D with numbered examples.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: The document addresses what constitutes reportable lobbying activity and costs when an employer has lobbyists influencing agencies, including salary reporting thresholds, reportable costs, and the circumstances under which an agency is deemed to be influenced.

CONCLUSION: FPPC provides that salary is reportable only if an employee spends 10% or more of compensated time influencing legislative or administrative action; all other costs for influencing an agency must be reported regardless; an agency is deemed influenced if a lobbyist or 10-percent employee influences it, but the staff has not decided whether a monetary threshold applies when time spent is below 10%.
embedding.first_500_words: tsb.LUY Influencing an Agency Assume that a son is a lobbyist employer: A. No portion of an employee's salary will be reportable unless that employee spends 10 percent or more of his compensated time during the month influencing legis- lative or administrative action. B. Even if no portion of an employee's salary is reportable, the employer must report all other costs incurred for the purpose of influencing an agency which his lobbyist influences as a substantial or regular part of his business. C. If an agency is not being influenced by the employer's lobbyist, the agency nevertheless 11 be deemed to have been influenced by the employer if an employee spends 10 percent or more of his time influencing all agencies spends a substantial or reg~lar part of h s time influencing the agency in question. D. If an agency is not being influenced by either the lobbyist or a 10-percenter, the staff has not decided whether the spending of a certain threshold amount of money in connection with influencing an agency will mean that the agency is being influenced pursuant to 18600. For example if an employee spends 3 percent of his time influencing Agency X but spends $10~ $200 or $300 in that effort, is the agency being influenced by the employer? 17D
embedding.summary: This FPPC advice letter addresses the reportability of employee salaries and costs related to lobbying activities, establishing a 10% time threshold for salary reporting and clarifying when agencies are deemed to be influenced by an employer's lobbying efforts.
